title: NQ Europe Large Offer Breakdown
focus_questions: Is the sell wall still controlling price on revisit?
Did the breakdown produce real downside travel or only visual pressure?

summary.thesis: A persistent upper sell wall plus real downside initiative can create a measured Europe-session breakdown whose origin later matters as resistance memory.
summary.operator_translation: Only treat the wall as actionable if price actually releases lower and the cap still matters on revisit.
summary.ai_usage_note: Surface this card when overhead liquidity, downside drive, and later revisit logic all appear in the same replay window.
summary.ai_brief: Environment: Europe-session pressure with a visible upper cap. Required evidence: persistent overhead liquidity plus downside initiative and measurable travel. Do not trade: if the wall is only visual or price is still balancing. Real trigger: release away from the cap with later revisit value. Failure point: acceptance above the old cap without renewed sell control.

when_relevant: A significant ask-side liquidity band remains above price during Europe trade.
Initiative selling expands price away from that cap.
The original cap zone is likely to matter again on revisit.
required_evidence: initiative_drive shows downside travel instead of immediate stall
the market leaves enough distance from the origin to create later revisit value
confirmation_signals: the wall stays close enough to keep capping price during the release
downside travel expands into measurable follow-through
revisit shows renewed rejection or rebuilt sell liquidity
invalidation_signals: price begins accepting above the original cap
the wall disappears without renewed rejection
downside initiative fails to produce meaningful travel
no_trade_conditions: Do not short only because a bright wall is visible if price has not actually released lower.
Do not short in the middle of balance when there is no clean downside travel away from the cap.
Do not keep pressing the short if price is already accepting above the old cap and the wall is no longer rebuilding.

entry_archetypes.name: release away from cap
entry_archetypes.description: Short after initiative selling proves the cap is producing actual downside travel.
entry_archetypes.risk_note: Avoid if price has not yet separated from the cap zone.

entry_archetypes.name: failed revisit of cap
entry_archetypes.description: Short on revisit when the old cap rebuilds and price fails to accept above it.
entry_archetypes.risk_note: Avoid if the revisit is already accepting above the prior wall.

management_notes: Track how far the breakdown traveled before looking for continuation.
A revisit matters more than the first move if the cap zone still has memory.
If the cap is reclaimed and accepted, resistance logic must be downgraded.
review_questions: Did the wall produce a real downside outcome, or did it only look heavy on screen?
Was the short taken after most of the measurable travel was already complete?
When price revisited the cap, did it reject again or begin accepting above it?
